zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden於大約1310年出生在英國時，他的父親William de Radcliffe43歲，母親Margaret De Hindley41歲。他和Ellen de Langfield至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1360年在英國越可爾享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden于大约1310年出生在英国时，他的父亲William de Radcliffe43岁，母亲Margaret De Hindley41岁。他和Ellen de Langfield至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1360年在英国越可爾享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden was born about 1310, in England, his father, William de Radcliffe, was 43 and his mother, Margaret De Hindley, was 41. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Ellen de Langfield. He died in 1360, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden est né environ 1310, à Angleterre, son père, William de Radcliffe, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Margaret De Hindley, avait 41 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Ellen de Langfield. Il est décédé en 1360, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden um 1310 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Radcliffe 43 und seine Mutter Margaret De Hindley 41. Er hatte mit Ellen de Langfield mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1360 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden è nato il circa 1310 nel England, il suo padre, William de Radcliffe, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Margaret De Hindley, aveva 41 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Ellen de Langfield. Morì nel 1360, nel Yorkshire, England, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmordenが約1310年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William de Radcliffeは43歳，母Margaret De Hindleyは41歳であった。彼はEllen de Langfieldとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1360年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden(이)가 대략 1310년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Radcliffe(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret De Hindley(은)는 41세였다. 그는 Ellen de Langfield(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1360년 영국 요르크실르에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Radcliffe, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Margaret De Hindley, tinha 41 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Ellen de Langfield. Ele faleceu em 1360, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden родился около 1310 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени William de Radcliffe было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret De Hindley, 41. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Ellen de Langfield. Он умер в 1360 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord William de Radcliffe of Todmorden nació aproximadamente 1310, en Inglaterra, su papá, William de Radcliffe, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Margaret De Hindley, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Ellen de Langfield. Murió en 1360, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años.
